title: My tax bill — what would I actually pay?
intro: Estimate the Town's portion of your property-tax bill using the Town's own published 2026 rate table. This covers only the Town — county, school, fire, and library taxes are billed separately and aren't included here.

rateSource.title: 2026 Adopted Budget
rateSource.note: Rate table, p. 6: 'Total Town Wide' rate, updated based on the final assessment roll.

equalization.source: Town of Riverhead 2025-2026 Receiver of Taxes rate sheet. Its residential assessment ratio (RAR) of 7.44% is the figure New York's Homestead Tax Option (RPTL Article 19) designates specifically for converting a residential property's market value to assessed value — more precise for this purpose than the town's general equalization/uniform percentage (about 8.16-8.34% depending on the roll year), which is meant for cross-jurisdiction comparison, not household-level conversion.
equalization.note: This ratio can shift slightly from year to year with each new assessment roll. Use it only as an approximation if you don't know your assessed value directly; if you have a recent tax bill or assessment notice, enter that assessed value instead for an exact answer.
